scope: External source prototype for testing PDD/Temu moat hypotheses that cannot be proven from official financial reports alone.

quality_tiers.label: official_or_regulator
quality_tiers.allowed_role: Can support facts about rules, filings, policies, enforcement, and disclosed business model constraints.

quality_tiers.label: reputable_aggregate_or_media
quality_tiers.allowed_role: Can support trend/context claims when methodology or editorial standard is visible.
examples: free industry reports with visible methodology

quality_tiers.label: direct_user_or_merchant_voice
quality_tiers.allowed_role: Can support pattern leads and customer/merchant sentiment themes, but not standalone important conclusions.

quality_tiers.label: anonymous_or_influencer_opinion
quality_tiers.allowed_role: Lead generation only unless repeated across higher-quality sources.

hypotheses.hypothesis: PDD/Temu may have a two-sided marketplace network effect between buyers and merchants.
hypotheses.official_report_basis: Official reports discuss buyers, merchants, platform/ecosystem language, online marketing services, and transaction services.
tests: Does user demand attract merchants without unsustainable subsidy?
Do merchants report valuable traffic and repeatable economics?
Do customers return because selection/value improves with more merchants?

hypotheses.hypothesis: PDD/Temu may have a durable cost/value advantage from merchant efficiency, supply-chain scale, and low-price positioning.
hypotheses.official_report_basis: Official reports discuss value-for-money, competitive prices, supply chain support, and merchant operating efficiency.
tests: Are low prices supported by structural efficiency rather than temporary subsidies?
Do customers accept product quality and delivery tradeoffs?
Can Alibaba, JD, Shein, Amazon, or Douyin-like competitors copy the same model?

hypotheses.hypothesis: PDD may have an engagement advantage from interactive or social shopping behavior.
hypotheses.official_report_basis: Official reports discuss interactive shopping and, historically, social/team-purchase language.
tests: Do users show habit/repeat use rather than one-time deal seeking?
Is engagement quality high, or driven by promotions and gamified friction?
Is the engagement advantage visible outside company language?

hypotheses.hypothesis: PDD/Temu may offer merchants a valuable demand-generation and operating-support channel.
hypotheses.official_report_basis: Official reports describe merchant services, online marketing services, transaction services, and platform support.
tests: Do sellers report profitable order flow after ads, discounts, returns, and logistics?
Do platform rules create fragile or adversarial merchant relationships?
Do seller experiences differ between Pinduoduo and Temu?

hypotheses.hypothesis: Customer trust, product quality, logistics, data/privacy, and trade regulation may weaken or cap the moat.
hypotheses.official_report_basis: Official reports identify competition, quality, logistics, regulatory, and trade risks.
tests: Are complaints isolated service issues or structural trust problems?
Do regulators or customs changes threaten the cross-border model?
Does quality perception prevent long-term customer loyalty?

source_lines.name: App reviews and app-store aggregate reception
source_lines.validation_role: Test customer satisfaction, trust, delivery, refund, quality, and repeat-use signals.

planned_sources.name: Apple App Store Temu reviews
planned_sources.locator: Apple App Store Temu app reviews rating complaints refund shipping quality
planned_sources.expected_evidence: Aggregate rating, review volume, recent review themes, and complaint clusters.

planned_sources.name: Google Play Temu reviews
planned_sources.locator: Google Play Temu reviews rating complaints refund shipping quality
planned_sources.expected_evidence: Aggregate rating, review volume, recent review themes, and complaint clusters.

audit_notes: Separate aggregate rating/volume from cherry-picked review examples.
Low-star review anecdotes cannot prove a broad moat problem without pattern evidence.

source_lines.name: Customer public discussion and product experience
source_lines.validation_role: Look for repeated real-user patterns around product quality, delivery time, refunds, app experience, and whether low price creates loyalty.

planned_sources.name: Reddit Temu customer discussions
planned_sources.locator: site:reddit.com Temu review quality refund shipping app experience repeat purchase
planned_sources.expected_evidence: Repeated user themes and disconfirming examples.

planned_sources.name: YouTube Temu product experience videos
planned_sources.locator: YouTube Temu review quality shipping refund haul repeat purchase
planned_sources.expected_evidence: Visible product/shipping/review patterns, with creator incentives flagged.

planned_sources.name: Bilibili / 知乎 / 小红书 Pinduoduo and Temu discussions
planned_sources.locator: 拼多多 Temu 质量 售后 物流 用户 评价 复购
planned_sources.expected_evidence: Chinese customer sentiment and product/service complaint themes.

audit_notes: Treat individual posts/videos as leads, not facts.
Important claims require repeated independent sources or stronger Tier 1/Tier 2 support.

source_lines.name: Merchant and seller economics
source_lines.validation_role: Test whether merchants receive durable value after platform rules, marketing spend, returns, fines, logistics, and required discounts.

planned_sources.name: Temu seller / merchant official pages
planned_sources.locator: Temu seller center merchant rules fees logistics returns official
planned_sources.expected_evidence: Official merchant rules, fee/logistics disclosures, and seller obligations.

planned_sources.name: Chinese merchant discussions
planned_sources.locator: 拼多多 商家 利润 罚款 退货 流量 广告 Temu 卖家 规则
planned_sources.expected_evidence: Repeated merchant claims about profitability, traffic quality, and platform-rule friction.

planned_sources.name: Seller forums and ecommerce communities
planned_sources.locator: Temu seller forum merchant profitability fees returns logistics complaints
planned_sources.expected_evidence: Seller economics themes and evidence of merchant satisfaction or churn risk.

audit_notes: Official seller pages can support rules but not seller profitability.
Merchant complaints need triangulation because dissatisfied sellers are overrepresented in forums.

source_lines.name: Competitor public materials
source_lines.validation_role: Test whether PDD's model is differentiated or easily copied by Alibaba, JD, Amazon, Shein, Douyin-like commerce, or other low-price platforms.

planned_sources.name: Alibaba annual reports and IR
planned_sources.locator: Alibaba annual report Taobao Tmall value-for-money marketplace merchant services
planned_sources.expected_evidence: Comparable marketplace model, merchant services, take-rate and competition language.

planned_sources.name: JD annual reports and IR
planned_sources.locator: JD.com annual report marketplace logistics low price merchant services
planned_sources.expected_evidence: Competitor logistics/supply-chain strengths and low-price positioning.

planned_sources.name: Amazon annual reports and marketplace seller policies
planned_sources.locator: Amazon annual report third-party seller marketplace low price selection fulfillment
planned_sources.expected_evidence: Comparable marketplace scale, fulfillment, seller economics, and selection language.

planned_sources.name: Shein public materials
planned_sources.locator: Shein marketplace supplier model low price cross-border ecommerce public report
planned_sources.expected_evidence: Private-company competitor context where reliable public material is available.

audit_notes: Competitors should eventually run through the same financial and business-model workflow before final comparison.
Private-company competitor claims require stricter source-quality labels.

source_lines.name: Regulatory, trade, and reputation risk
source_lines.validation_role: Test whether customs, de minimis rules, data/privacy, consumer protection, product safety, or enforcement issues could weaken the model.

planned_sources.name: U.S. customs and de minimis policy sources
planned_sources.locator: site:cbp.gov de minimis ecommerce imports Temu PDD
planned_sources.expected_evidence: Official rule changes or enforcement context affecting cross-border low-price commerce.

planned_sources.name: FTC / consumer protection sources
planned_sources.locator: site:ftc.gov Temu consumer protection refund privacy ecommerce
planned_sources.expected_evidence: Official actions, warnings, or policy context.

planned_sources.name: EU consumer protection / DSA / customs sources
planned_sources.locator: site:ec.europa.eu Temu consumer protection Digital Services Act customs ecommerce
planned_sources.expected_evidence: Official EU regulatory context for marketplace obligations.

planned_sources.name: Chinese complaint and consumer platforms
planned_sources.locator: 黑猫投诉 拼多多 Temu 售后 退款 质量
planned_sources.expected_evidence: Complaint patterns that need triangulation against stronger sources.

audit_notes: Official regulator sources can support facts about rules/actions.
Complaint platforms show complaint patterns, not population-wide rates.

source_lines.name: Independent business reporting and free industry context
source_lines.validation_role: Provide context, contradictions, and leads for unit economics, platform strategy, merchant incentives, and regulation.

planned_sources.name: Reputable business media on Temu/PDD model
planned_sources.locator: PDD Temu business model merchant economics supply chain low price reputable media
planned_sources.expected_evidence: Reported details and contradictions to official company framing.

planned_sources.name: Chinese business media and public industry analysis
planned_sources.locator: 拼多多 Temu 商业模式 商家 供应链 低价 行业 分析
planned_sources.expected_evidence: Local-language business context and merchant/supply-chain detail.

audit_notes: Media claims need source attribution and should not override official financial facts.
Free industry reports must expose methodology before being used for important claims.

review_needed_decisions.decision: V1 records external source lines and search tasks, but does not yet scrape or summarize public discussion automatically.
review_needed_decisions.reason: This prevents noisy low-quality sources from being promoted into moat conclusions before the audit policy is tested.

review_needed_decisions.decision: Tier 3 and Tier 4 sources may only create leads/patterns unless repeated across independent sources or supported by Tier 1/Tier 2 evidence.
review_needed_decisions.reason: Customer and merchant forums have selection bias and cannot safely prove important investment claims by themselves.

review_needed_decisions.decision: Use both English and Chinese source lines for PDD/Temu because customer, merchant, and competitor evidence differs by geography.
review_needed_decisions.reason: PDD spans Pinduoduo in China and Temu internationally, so English-only research would miss important merchant/customer evidence.

review_needed_decisions.decision: Competitor materials are source candidates here; final competitor comparison still requires each competitor to go through the same agent workflow.
review_needed_decisions.reason: A shortcut competitor scan is useful for leads but not enough for a final moat judgment.

auditor_rules: Do not use external sources for official financial numbers.
Do not make final moat conclusions from Tier 3 or Tier 4 sources alone.
Mark every external evidence item with source tier, source type, language, and whether it supports, contradicts, or only raises a question.
Important claims from low-quality sources require human review.
Contradictory evidence should be preserved rather than averaged away.
